background: Assume that the product failures from an assembly line are 5%. Suppose that if a product is defective, a diagnostic test indicates that it is defective 92% of the time, and if the product is good, the test indicates that it is good 96% of the time.

task.query: Calculate the probability that the product is good given that the test indicates defective.
task.answer: Let A = product is good (not defective) A_c = product is defective B = a test indicates that it is good (not defective) B_c = a test indicates it is defective Then P(A)=0.95, P(A_c)=0.05; P(B | A)=0.96, P(B_c | A_c)=0.92 The probability that the product is good given that the test indicates defective is: P(A | B_c) = P(A ∩ B_c) / P(B_c) = [P(B_c | A) * P(A)] / [P(B_c | A) * P(A) + P(B_c | A_c) * P(A_c)] = (0.04 * 0.95) / [(0.04 * 0.95) + (0.92 * 0.05)] = 0.4524
